SCOPE.CTN 0.0.1
  • Reference
    • Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
    • Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
    • Prefiltering cell type triads using CLQ permutation test

Prefiltering cell type triads using CLQ permutation test

Triad_prefilter_CLQ.Rmd
library(SCOPE.CTN)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)

SCOPE identifies niches driven by cell-type triads. However, as the number of cell types grows, the combinations of three cell types increase exponentially. To reduce the computational burden, we utilize a colocation quotient (CLQ) permutation test to prioritize cell type triads that exhibit significant spatial proximity.

data.frame(n_celltype = 3:50, n_combn = choose(3:50, 3)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_celltype, y = n_combn)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  theme_cowplot() + 
  labs(x = "Number of Cell Types", y = "Number of triads")

Following Bouchard et al., the colocalization of a pair of cell types aa and bb in tissue section ll can be evaluated with metric

CLQb→a(l)={Cb→a(l)/Na(l)Nb(l)/(N(l)−1)Na(l)>5 andNb(l)>50,otherwise,CLQ_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l)} = \begin{cases} \frac{C_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l)} / N_a^{(l)}}{N_b^{(l)} / (N^{(l)} - 1)} & N_a^{(l)} > 5 \mbox{ and} \ N_b^{(l)} > 5 \\ 0, & \text{otherwise}\end{cases},

where CLQb→a(l)CLQ_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l)} is the number of cells of cell type bb within the neighborhood of cell type aa; Na(l)N_a^{(l)}, Nb(l)N_b^{(l)}, and N(l)N^{(l)} are the number of cells for cell type aa, bb and the total number of cells in tissue section ll, respectively.

Here we use the 45-sample pulmonary fibrosis (PF) Xenium dataset from Vannan et al. Nat Genet 2025 to demonstrate how to perform CLQ permutation test. This dataset contains 47 cell types, which can form 16,215 potential triads.

For each tissue section, we first calculate an observed CLQ value CLQb→a(l),obsCLQ_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l),obs} using the true cell type labels. Please refer to the Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data to see how PF_Xenium_dat_in and PF_Xenium_cell_neighbor_results were obtained.

# Load data
data("PF_Xenium_dat_in")
data("PF_Xenium_cell_neighbor_results")

# Neighborhood cell counts
cell_neighbor_counts <- lapply(
  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "count.df") %>%
  do.call("rbind", .)
# Nearest neighbor cell ids
cell_neighbor_ids <- lapply(
  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "count.df") %>%
  do.call("rbind", .)
CLQ_obs <- compute_CLQ_observed(
  cell_neighbor_counts = cell_neighbor_counts,
  dat_in = dat_in)

head(CLQ_obs)

We then randomly shuffle the cell type labels for 500 times to create a null distribution of CLQs assuming that the cell types are distributed randomly. CLQb→a(l),k,k=1,2,…,500CLQ_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l),k},k = 1,2,…,500.

CLQ_perm <- compute_CLQ_permutated(
  cell_neighbor_ids = cell_neighbor_ids, dat_in = dat_in,
  iter.num = 500, seed = 42, n_cores = 5)

The permutation test p-value is defined as

pb→a(l)=∑k=1500I(CLQb→a(l),k>CLQb→a(l),obs),p_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l)} = \sum_{k=1}^{500}{I\left(CLQ_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l), k} > CLQ_{b\rightarrow a}^{(l), obs}\right)},

where II is the indicator function.

CLQ_perm_pval <- CLQ_permutation_test(CLQ_obs = CLQ_obs, CLQ_perm = CLQ_perm)

The function returns both nominal and Benjamini-Hochberg (BH) adjusted p-values. We prioritize cell type triads all constituent pairs exhibit significant CLQs (adjusted p-value < 0.05) in at least 10 tissue sections.

## Exclude cell types with too few cells
celltype_to_exclude <- table(dat_in$Celltype)[table(dat_in$Celltype) < 500]

## All cell type combinations
celltype_combs <- combn(setdiff(levels(dat_in$Celltype), celltype_to_exclude), 3) %>%
  t() %>% as.data.frame()
celltype_combs_signif <- celltype_combs %>%
  left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V1" = "Celltype", "V2" = "Celltype_neighbor")) %>%
  left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V1" = "Celltype", "V3" = "Celltype_neighbor", "ImageID"),
            suffix = c(".1", "")) %>%
  left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V2" = "Celltype", "V3" = "Celltype_neighbor", "ImageID"),
            suffix = c(".2", ".3")) 

combination_table_filtered = celltype_combs_signif %>%
  filter(p_perm.adj.1 < 0.05 & p_perm.adj.2 < 0.05 & p_perm.adj.3 < 0.05) %>%
  group_by(V1, V2, V3) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  filter(n >= 10) %>%
  select(-n)

print("Number of cell type triads: ", nrow(combination_table_filtered))
sessionInfo()
